Claimable preflight publication could occur after the shared deadline because diagnostics and latest publication separated the last clock read from the preflight rename; a signal after rename but before the committed flag could also pair claimable success with a signal exit.

The locked required-command mutant altered timeout clamping instead of proving that a swallowed command failure and synthetic success remain rejected.

Fresh client-side CI polling and a matched head SHA could not prevent a newly pending check from racing an unprotected GitHub merge; server-enforced strict CI protection was absent.

Terminal commit trusted candidate-ledger, staged-ledger, report, monitor, and latest hashes captured before latest publication instead of revalidating every bound file under the terminal lock immediately before canonical-ledger rename.

Terminal staging copied provisional zero-open resolved rows into stable run-local issues.jsonl before canonical-ledger rename, so a signal or timeout could leave success-shaped run evidence without committed terminal authority.

The terminal record and recovery bound latest.json ledger and proof claims but not its remaining metadata, allowing branch, path, or other success fields to change after staging without invalidating the canonical commit.

The workflow required classic branch-protection context CI / Verify even though GitHub Actions emits check-run context Verify and exposes CI only as separate workflow metadata, making the protected merge unsatisfiable.

Merge protection was first validated after commit, push, ready pull-request creation, and CI, so a known protection failure consumed the one preflight claim and performed remote writes before rejection.
A TERM recorded after the terminal signal check but before the bounded ledger worker installed its traps could still launch the worker, commit the canonical ledger, and exit zero.
A failed terminal commit could publish failure latest evidence by exiting from the EXIT trap before cleanup, leaving a success-shaped issues.jsonl.terminal staging file in stable run evidence.

Strict administrator-enforced context Verify remained source-unbound because both protection validators ignored checks[].app_id, allowing another producer to satisfy the server rule instead of GitHub Actions app 15368.

Two exact focused-suite runs failed different process-heavy launcher harnesses only under loaded-host scheduling while both passed immediately in isolation; a 300ms success deadline and Bun's default 5s test deadline made deterministic preflight flaky without exercising production timing semantics.

A signal could arrive after the terminal worker's final pre-spawn signal check but before the parent captured its PID, allowing the untracked worker to commit the canonical ledger before ambiguity recovery converted its signal status into success.

The preflight-order test accepted a missing merge-protection call because index -1 still satisfied the less-than ordering assertion, so deleting the required protection gate did not fail the deterministic suite.

Bounded output capture used command substitution, so Bash could defer the parent TERM, INT, or HUP trap until the substitution returned and let a timed-out or signaled command outlive its launcher deadline before cleanup.

A failed or lost gh pr merge response threw before its CommandLog was retained and before GitHub state was queried, so an exactly matched pull request that had merged could be reported as a failed run.

A signal or deadline expiry after terminal ledger staging could leave the success-shaped terminal ledger stage in stable run evidence because signal cleanup invalidated prior evidence before removing that stage.

remaining_launcher_ms called Bun through command substitution during active child polling; Bash deferred the parent signal trap until the clock subprocess returned, so TERM took 2174ms against the 1500ms bound and the active child outlived timely cleanup.

terminal_commit_owned became true before latest and preflight renames; TERM immediately before first publication was only recorded, then both success artifacts published and the finalizer exited 0 instead of 143.

Semantic positive-control taint retained only the allowed production file path, so a passing control could call one exported function while the additive RED assertion called a different export from the same file and still authorize reproduction.

The concurrent same-ID ledger conflict harness used a fixed 100ms sleep, so loaded-host scheduling could let the child remove or finish with the lock before the parent injected the concurrent source suffix, producing ENOENT instead of testing the conflict contract.

Semantic production taint was keyed by identifier text instead of lexical symbol identity, so a nested declaration or untainted reassignment could shadow a tainted binding while the RED assertion still inherited the control's production origin.

Additive RED validation compared parsed statement text instead of requiring one exact raw insertion, so baseline trivia or disabling directive tokens such as eslint-disable or @ts-* outside the added test could change without invalidating reproduction.

Preflight signal cleanup failed when both canonical quarantine paths and reused private fallbacks were occupied, leaving canonical preflight and latest success claimable instead of clearing or reallocating fresh current-run fallbacks, retrying retraction, and verifying canonical success absent.

Semantic RED validation accepted expect(actual).not() as a matcher call, so Bun could throw an unrelated TypeError while the marker-bearing test name still satisfied RED output instead of proving a production-bound assertion failure.

run_before_deadline returned status 0 after its group leader exited while a background descendant remained; a delayed-write harness observed immediate=false and later=true, allowing post-success mutation of package lock or evidence after final hashing.

Semantic positive-control validation accepted a bare expect(productionResult) plus an unrelated passing matcher, so a tautology could make the control green without any genuine Bun matcher asserting the production value.

RED validation accepted a passing marker-bearing target when an unrelated test in the same command failed, because the marker could appear anywhere in combined output instead of on the target's own Bun failure record.

The workflow decoded baseline test bytes with a TextDecoder configuration that stripped a UTF-8 BOM, so candidate source could remove those baseline bytes and still satisfy the claimed exact raw additive insertion proof.

A command descendant called setsid before its leader exited, escaped the original process group, and performed a delayed write after run_before_deadline returned success; PGID-only TERM and KILL could not prove command-owned descendants quiescent.

Positive-control analysis accepted a production-bound matcher after an unconditional return, so the runtime control could pass an unrelated assertion and exit before executing any causal assertion over the production value.

RED failure provenance used substring marker matching, so a passing target marked ORCA_RED:candidate-x plus an unrelated failing test marked ORCA_RED:candidate-x-extra could be accepted as the target regression failure.

Command-owner inspection added a full same-user environment scan after every bounded command; its measured 60-80 millisecond cost accumulated across finalizer harness commands and made the default artifact gate fail with ten test timeouts instead of completing deterministically.

Command-owner inspection redirected every same-user process command and environment into a private temporary file; abrupt SIGKILL could bypass best-effort wiping and leave unrelated credentials persisted on disk instead of retaining only matched owner PIDs.

Semantic control reachability treated a production-bound matcher after a labeled break as executable, so an unrelated assertion could pass while the causal matcher was skipped inside nested labeled blocks.

Semantic control analysis descended into optional-call arguments and optional-element indexes and accepted optional production calls even though short-circuiting could prevent the matcher or production observation from executing.

Semantic control analysis recorded declaration results without tracking nested writes or invoked local behavior, so assignments, getters, constructors, class evaluation, callbacks, or other evaluated setup could overwrite a production-derived value with the expected literal before assertion.

Candidate RED validation was weaker than positive-control validation, allowing a marker-bearing test with overwritten or discarded production values, unrelated failure paths, a bogus matcher chain, or a different production entrypoint to count as the regression proof.

The candidate RED marker could already exist in baseline test source, so later marker-bound output did not prove that the newly added regression test supplied the observed failure.

Semantic control analysis lost the production origin through await for named and namespace calls, rejecting legitimate async observations while lacking an explicit fail-closed boundary for optional awaited production calls.

The RED gate ran the whole candidate test file instead of an anchored escaped selector for the discovered RED test name, so unrelated tests could execute and supply the failing result.

RED evidence accepted marker presence without one exact failing Bun reporter record and a unique canonical one-test summary, allowing wrong or prefix names, pass-plus-unrelated-fail output, skip or todo records, duplicate records, injected line breaks, and contradictory counts.

Matcher-argument passivity followed const bindings without requiring their initializer to resolve to a primitive, so an effectful Proxy-backed expected value could execute custom comparison behavior and forge semantic proof.

The reproduction prompt required the full targeted test file to fail even after parent RED evidence was bound to an anchored exact-name selector, allowing agent-side RED instructions and authoritative proof commands to diverge.

Const did not make array or object containers immutable, and toBeOneOf delegated to mutable Array.prototype.includes, so container mutation or prototype replacement could forge matcher outcomes while remaining on the allowlist.
